page: Amazon Web Services x8i.2xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it features 8 dedicated vCPUs derived from 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor runs at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz and is equipped with a large 480 MB L3 cache. It is configured with 128.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding 16.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core capabilities. The instance achieves a top-tier Geekbench single-core score of 2171.0 and a text processing score of 2975.0, both placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Single-threaded data processing is also strong, with gzip compression reaching 34.38 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.52 GB/sec. Multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks generally fall within the average range, with uncached memory read bandwidth at 75.1 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 48.77 GB/sec. For machine learning workloads, the CPU's Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) support strong text generation speeds of 304.11 tokens/sec on small 135M LLM models, though performance drops to a weaker 8.63 tokens/sec on medium 7B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the x8i.2xlarge offers a high density of DDR5 memory relative to its compute footprint, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel CPU scaling. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs means users must rely on network-attached storage, positioning this instance for workloads that are memory-intensive rather than storage-I/O or graphics-heavy. It is well-suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, text processing pipelines, and moderate enterprise applications.
description: The Amazon Web Services x8i.2xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized server powered by an 8-vCPU Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing a high 16.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. While it lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core execution, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2171.0 and strong single-threaded compression speeds. Advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX accelerate low-parameter LLM inference. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, low-vCPU applications, making it highly suitable for in-memory databases, caching systems, and text-processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x8i.2xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 8 vCPUs, an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor, and 128.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, designed for memory-intensive workloads on the Nitro hypervisor.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x8i.2xlarge is an 8-vCPU memory-optimized server with 128.0 GB of DDR5 RAM and an Intel Xeon processor running at 3.9 GHz.
tagline: A memory-optimized server featuring high-speed DDR5 RAM, dedicated Intel Xeon processing, and top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor at 3.9 GHz
128.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory providing 16.0 GB per vCPU
Top-tier single-core performance and single-threaded data compression
Intel AMX and AVX-512 extensions for accelerated vector and matrix operations
No local storage or GPU, utilizing a 4 Gbps baseline network interface
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching layers, and text processing
